sales.description: Retail sales records containing transaction details, customer information, and revenue by department.

columns.Customer Name: Full name of the buyer. Watch out for auto-generated test data (e.g., 'Customer_123').
columns.Customer Email: Buyer's contact email. Blank or invalid formats are data quality issues.
columns.Amount: Total revenue from the sale. Must be a positive number; negative values are anomalies.

accrual_accounts.description: Financial accounting records showing pending, back-posted, or cleared accrual transactions.

columns.Unnamed: 0: Data export artifact (row index). IGNORE this column in all analyses.
columns.Authorization Group: Security or department code for the transaction.
columns.Bus. Transac. Type: The specific category of the business transaction.
columns.Calculate Tax: Indicator if tax calculation was applied.
Flag showing if this transaction impacts the company's cash flow.
columns.Cleared Item: Status indicating if the accounting balance has been settled/reconciled.
columns.Clearing Date: The date when the transaction was officially settled.
columns.Country Key: Geographic code for the transaction origin.
columns.Currency: The currency of the transaction (e.g., USD, EUR).
columns.Debit/Credit ind: Indicator showing if the transaction is a Debit (D) or Credit (C).
columns.Transaction Value: The monetary amount of the transaction. Note: Negative values typically represent debits, refunds, or reversals, which is normal in accounting but should be highlighted.
columns.Document Is Back-Posted: Flag indicating if the document was posted retroactively into a closed period.
columns.Exchange rate: Currency exchange rate applied, if not in the base currency.
columns.Fiscal Year.1: Primary financial year for this accounting record.
